heading: Export Management
body:
To export your Cloud Backup snapshots, you need an `M10` or
higher Atlas cluster with Cloud Backup [Cloud Backup](backup-cloud-provider). In addition, to export to an object store,
you must do the following:

- Configure AWS (Amazon Web Services) [IAM role](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_use.html)
  with `STS:AssumeRole` that grants Atlas access to your AWS (Amazon Web Services)
  resources. To learn more about configuring AWS (Amazon Web Services) access for
  Atlas, see [Set Up Unified AWS Access](set-up-unified-aws-access).

- Configure AWS (Amazon Web Services) [IAM role policy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access_policies.html)
  that grants Atlas write access or the `S3:PutObject` and
  `S3:GetBucketLocation` permissions to your AWS (Amazon Web Services) resources. To
  learn more about configuring write access to AWS (Amazon Web Services) resources, see
  [Set Up Unified AWS Access](set-up-unified-aws-access).

  > **Example:** 
  >
  > ```json
  > {
  >   "Version": "2012-10-17",
  >   "Statement": [
  >     {
  >       "Effect": "Allow",
  >       "Action": "s3:GetBucketLocation",
  >       "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::bucket-name"
  >     },
  >     {
  >       "Effect": "Allow",
  >       "Action": "s3:PutObject",
  >       "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::bucket-name/*"
  >     }
  >   ]
  > }
  > ```
